skill.name: create-feature
skill.description: Coordinates full feature development from requirement analysis through backend, frontend, architecture review, QA checks, and pull request creation.
skill.summary: Orchestrates multi-step feature delivery with specialist development, review, QA, and PR workflows.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown code fences and slash-command examples as shell backtick execution; these are mostly false positives, not executable Ruby or shell code. The weak cryptography and entropy alerts are also false positives based on the inspected SKILL.md context. The skill still has medium operational risk because it directs autonomous repository modification, command/tool execution, external resource use, and GitHub pull request creation.

medium_findings.title: Broad Autonomous Repository Modification
medium_findings.description: The skill directs backend, frontend, database, documentation, and test changes, then repeats fixes until review and QA pass. This appears legitimate for a feature-development skill, but it can make wide repository changes and should require normal workspace and git safeguards. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The referenced lines explicitly list generated application files, migrations, documentation updates, tests, and retry loops; no malicious intent was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly performs broad code, database, documentation, and test edits. The risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: External Resource and Pull Request Actions
medium_findings.description: The skill mentions external resource retrieval for Figma and Context7 and later creates a GitHub pull request. These actions are expected for this workflow but may send project context to external services or publish changes. Confidence: 0.82. Confidence reasoning: The file directly names external resource retrieval and GitHub PR creation, but there is no evidence of hidden exfiltration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The external interactions are described plainly in the workflow. The evidence supports a warning, not a block.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Alerts Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the inspected locations are Markdown code fences, a git branch example, slash-command invocations, documentation references, or report templates. These are not executable code in this skill file. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The cited SKILL.md lines show documentation syntax and command examples rather than embedded Ruby or shell backtick execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious backticks are Markdown formatting. I did not find executable Ruby backtick usage.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at lines 3, 70, and 71. The inspected text is a Japanese description and documentation filenames, not cryptographic code or algorithms. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or crypto API usage appears at the cited lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain prose and documentation names. I did not find evidence of weak cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alert Is Explained by Japanese Prose
low_findings.description: The entropy alert at the start of the file is consistent with front matter and Japanese natural language, not binary, encrypted, or obfuscated content. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The inspected file is readable Markdown with no encoded payload or hidden instruction block.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The content is human-readable Markdown. I did not find obfuscation or encrypted data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command and Skill Orchestration
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow includes a git branch command and multiple slash-command calls to other skills. These commands are documented and expected, but they can affect a repository when executed by an agent.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file directly shows the orchestration commands. The risk depends on execution context and referenced skills.

dangerous_patterns.title: Automated Pull Request Creation
dangerous_patterns.description: The workflow creates a GitHub pull request after implementation and QA. This is normal for the stated purpose, but it can publish generated changes externally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The PR creation step is explicit. No hidden or deceptive network behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Feature work often stalls between requirements, implementation, review, QA, and pull request preparation. This skill coordinates those steps through specialist workflows for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code users.
actual_capabilities: Collects feature name, issue number, specification path, Figma URL, and target implementation area.
Coordinates requirement analysis before implementation begins.
Runs backend and frontend specialist workflows when the selected target requires them.
Routes completed work through architecture review and QA checks.
Repeats implementation fixes when review or QA fails.
Creates a pull request and final completion report after quality checks pass.
limitations: Depends on separate specialist skills being installed and working correctly.
Assumes the repository uses compatible backend, frontend, testing, and documentation conventions.
May require access to Figma, Context7, GitHub, and project credentials.
Does not replace human review for security, product fit, or production readiness.

use_cases.title: Ship a Full Stack Feature
use_cases.description: Coordinate requirement analysis, backend work, frontend work, QA, and PR creation for a tracked issue.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Delivery
use_cases.description: Apply the same ordered workflow for implementation, architecture review, and QA across feature branches.

use_cases.title: Prepare Reviewable Pull Requests
use_cases.description: Generate implementation artifacts, run checks, and produce a final report before requesting review.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Simple Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Begin a feature from an existing issue.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use create-feature for feature_name="user profile" issue_number=123 target="fullstack". Analyze requirements, implement the feature, run QA, and create a PR.

prompt_templates.title: Build Backend Only
prompt_templates.scenario: Implement API and database work without frontend changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use create-feature for feature_name="invoice export" issue_number=241 specification_path="docs/specs/invoice-export.md" target="backend".

prompt_templates.title: Implement From Design
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a Figma design for frontend or full stack delivery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use create-feature for feature_name="settings dashboard" issue_number=318 figma_url="[Figma URL]" target="frontend". Follow the review and QA phases.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Complete Guarded Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Deliver a feature with strict checks and a final status report.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use create-feature for feature_name="team invitations" issue_number=456 specification_path="docs/specs/team-invitations.md" figma_url="[Figma URL]" target="fullstack". Stop and report if external access or credentials are missing.

output_examples.input: Create a full stack user profile feature for issue 123.
output_examples.output: The skill analyzes requirements, coordinates backend and frontend implementation, runs architecture review and QA, creates a pull request, and reports implemented APIs, pages, tests, coverage, and PR URL.

output_examples.input: Create a backend-only invoice export feature from a specification file.
output_examples.output: The skill focuses on backend artifacts such as controller, service, mapper, database migration, tests, documentation updates, QA results, and pull request creation.

output_examples.input: Create a frontend settings page from a Figma design.
output_examples.output: The skill coordinates design-aware frontend implementation, component structure, API integration preparation, tests, Storybook stories, QA checks, and a completion report.

best_practices: Run it on a clean feature branch with a linked issue number.
Confirm referenced specialist skills and project documentation exist before starting.
Review generated changes and the pull request before merging.
anti_patterns: Do not run it on protected branches or dirty worktrees.
Do not provide private design or repository data unless external access is approved.
Do not treat passing QA checks as a substitute for human code review.

faq.question: What does this skill coordinate?
faq.answer: It coordinates requirement analysis, backend work, frontend work, architecture review, QA checks, pull request creation, and final reporting.

faq.question: Can it build only backend or frontend work?
faq.answer: Yes. The target parameter controls whether it runs backend, frontend, or full stack implementation phases.

faq.question: Does it create a pull request?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow includes a create-pr phase after review and QA checks pass.

faq.question: Does it require other skills?
faq.answer: Yes. It references analyze-requirements, develop-backend, develop-frontend, review-architecture, qa-check, and create-pr.

faq.question: Why is there a medium security warning?
faq.answer: The skill can direct repository changes, command execution, external resource access, and GitHub pull request creation.

faq.question: Should users review the output?
faq.answer: Yes. Users should review code, tests, generated documentation, and the pull request before merging.
